summary: Iteration 29 - Tested NEW Revenue Analytics & Royalty Calculator feature. All 15 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend tests passed. Revenue Analytics page at /revenue shows 4 summary cards, Monthly Revenue Trend chart, Earnings by Platform bar chart + table, What-If Calculator with platform mix inputs, and Per-Stream Rate Guide with 8 platforms. Calculator correctly calculates projected earnings based on stream count and platform distribution.

issues: Console warnings about chart width/height being -1 during initial render - cosmetic only, charts render correctly after mount

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue requires authentication (returns 401/422 without token)
Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue returns summary with total_streams, gross_revenue, platform_fee, net_revenue, artist_take, collab_payouts, plan, avg_rate_per_stream
Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue returns platforms array with platform, streams, rate_per_stream, gross_revenue, net_revenue fields
Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue returns monthly_trend with 6 months of data (month, streams, gross, net)
Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue returns collaborator_splits array
Backend: GET /api/analytics/revenue correctly calculates platform_fee based on plan (pro = 0%)
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator requires authentication
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator accepts streams count and returns platform_breakdown, gross_revenue, net_revenue, artist_take
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator uses default mix (Spotify 45%, Apple Music 25%, YouTube Music 15%, Amazon Music 10%, Other 5%)
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator applies custom platform_mix correctly
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator platform_breakdown has correct structure (platform, streams, rate, gross)
Backend: POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator math verified (10000 Spotify streams = $40 gross)
Backend regression: GET /api/analytics/overview still works
Backend regression: POST /api/ai/release-strategy still works
Backend: Platform rates match expected values (Spotify $0.004, Apple Music $0.008, etc.)
Frontend: /revenue page loads with 'Revenue Analytics' title
Frontend: 4 summary cards shown (Gross Revenue, Platform Fee, Net Revenue, Your Take) with data-testid
Frontend: Monthly Revenue Trend area chart displays
Frontend: Earnings by Platform horizontal bar chart + table with streams, rate, gross, net columns
Frontend: What-If Calculator section with streams input, platform mix inputs, and Calculate button
Frontend: Calculator shows results after clicking Calculate (4 result cards + platform breakdown table)
Frontend: Per-Stream Rate Guide shows 8 platforms with rates
Frontend: Revenue nav item in sidebar links to /revenue with CurrencyDollar icon
critical_code_review_comments: Revenue calculation correctly uses SUBSCRIPTION_PLANS to determine platform cut (pro=0%, free=15%)
Platform rates are industry-standard: Spotify $0.004, Apple Music $0.008, YouTube $0.002, Amazon $0.004, Tidal $0.012, Deezer $0.003, Pandora $0.002, SoundCloud $0.003
Calculator correctly applies custom platform_mix percentages
Monthly trend aggregates last 6 months of stream_events data
Collaborator splits pulled from collaborations collection (status=accepted)
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - used existing stream_events data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 29 tested the NEW Revenue Analytics & Royalty Calculator feature. Backend endpoints: GET /api/analytics/revenue (returns summary, platforms array, monthly_trend, collaborator_splits), POST /api/analytics/revenue/calculator (what-if calculator with streams and optional platform_mix). Frontend: Revenue Analytics page at /revenue with 4 summary cards (data-testid='summary-*'), Monthly Revenue Trend chart, Earnings by Platform section (data-testid='platform-breakdown'), What-If Calculator (data-testid='revenue-calculator'), and Per-Stream Rate Guide (data-testid='rate-*'). Platform rates: Spotify $0.004, Apple Music $0.008, YouTube $0.002, Amazon $0.004, Tidal $0.012, Deezer $0.003, Pandora $0.002, SoundCloud $0.003. Admin user is on 'pro' plan with 0% platform fee.

response.platforms: array of {platform, streams, rate_per_stream, gross_revenue, net_revenue}
response.monthly_trend: array of 6 months {month, streams, gross, net}
response.collaborator_splits: array of {collaborator, release_id, role, split_percentage, estimated_amount}

components: Earnings by Platform bar chart + table (data-testid='platform-breakdown', 'platform-table')
